{{Quote|Grommet. You maladjusted sack of gravel worms. Are you making trouble again?|[[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]]|<ref name="TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide">''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>}}
'''Carith''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Flight officer/Legends|Flight Officer]], nicknamed '''Grommet''', who was befriended by [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]]. Grommet first met Stele aboard the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] ''[[Fogger]]'' during a series of Imperial attacks against Alliance forces that culminated in the destruction of the Calamari Light Cruiser [[Lulsla (MC40a light cruiser)|''Lulsla'']].

Grommet was the unofficial leader of a close knit group of pilots composed of himself, and the pilots [[Alimet]] and [[Ketcher]]. All three were close friends and usually served on the same ships. They were known to have served alongside Maarek Stele on the Fogger, and again onboard the [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]] ''[[Ludwick]]'' as part of an operation by [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] to construct the defense platform [[NL-1]] in the [[Pakuuni system/Legends|Pakuuni system]].

Grommet tended to be somewhat mischievous and occasionally led his informal group of pilots into trouble. During their tour of duty on the ''Ludwick'', he convinced Alimet, Ketcher and Stele to accompany him on an unauthorized trip to the ship's hangar during a visit by Vice Admiral Thrawn. They were eventually caught and, as punishment, were ordered by their commanding officer, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Buckeye]], to clean up a pirate base after its capture by the Empire.

At some point between his tour of duty aboard the ''Ludwick'' and the development of the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] prototype by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]], Grommet met a female Alliance officer while he was on leave. He quickly fell in love and eventually decided to defect to the Alliance. During a trip planetside, Grommet went AWOL and was never heard from again. This news came as a shock to his former friends, Maarek Stele and Alimet. Stele in particular was already experiencing doubts about the methods and motivation behind the Empire and the idea that he might be forced to shoot down a friend disturbed him deeply.
